name: gate
description: portitor's push-verification core: the checks a git pre-receive runs against an incoming push, inspecting the resulting objects/refs (not commands). Generic git-gateway logic with no beads/spex knowledge. NOTE: this module.json is a hand-authored SEED in spexmachina's format — identity hashes (requirement id/preq_id, component spec_node_id) and spec/.snapshot.json are generated when run through `spex` / the /spec skill; they are intentionally omitted here.

requirements.title: Restrict ref updates to the branch namespace
requirements.description: Every ref update must target refs/heads/<name> (non-empty name). Any other namespace — refs/tags/*, refs/notes/*, and especially refs/replace/* (whose objects substitute for others in later git reads) — is a 'ref-namespace' violation, for creates, updates, and deletions alike; a refused ref gets no further evaluation.

requirements.title: Reject pushes to the default branch
requirements.description: A ref update targeting refs/heads/<default> (push or deletion) is a violation. The default branch is taken from Config.DefaultBranch, else derived from the receiving repo's HEAD symref.

requirements.title: Require every new commit to be signed by an allowed signer
requirements.description: Each commit an update introduces (git rev-list old..new, or new --not --all on create) must have git signature verdict %G? == G against an allowed_signers file. None (N), bad (B), untrusted/not-in-allowed-signers (U), or error (E) is a violation.

requirements.title: Attribute each commit to a role by signing-key fingerprint
requirements.description: The signer key fingerprint (git %GF) maps to a role via Config.Roles (fingerprint -> role). Identity is a credential, not a label: a container holding only one role's key cannot sign as another role. Untrusted commits (verdict != G) are rejected before role evaluation.

requirements.title: Enforce role-gated content rules
requirements.description: A RoleRule {Name, PathGlob, AddedRegex, AllowedRoles} requires that any introduced commit whose diff to PathGlob adds a line matching AddedRegex be signed by a role in AllowedRoles, else a violation named Name. Generic mechanism; domain rules (e.g. 'only reviewer/owner may close a bead' on .beads/issues.jsonl) are supplied as external config, not built in.

requirements.title: Require feature branches to be based on the current default (opt-in)
requirements.description: When Config.RequireUpToDateWithDefault is set, a non-default ref update whose new tip is not a descendant of the current default branch (git merge-base --is-ancestor <default> <new>) is a 'stale-base' violation, forcing a fresh rebase before landing. Disabled by default; the default branch itself is exempt.

requirements.title: Forward accepted feature branches to the real upstream
requirements.description: post-receive (gate.Forward) pushes each accepted, non-default, non-deletion ref update to the configured upstream remote (Config.UpstreamRemote, default 'upstream') using credentials held only by the proxy. The default branch is never forwarded (PR/owner territory; pre-receive rejects pushes to it). Forwarding runs only after pre-receive has accepted the push.

requirements.title: Provision a gated bare repo
requirements.description: `portitor init-repo --bare <path> [--default <branch>] [--upstream <url>] [--config <json>]` creates a bare repo, optionally adds and fetches an upstream remote and seeds the default branch from it (so agents clone the default from the proxy with no upstream credential), and installs pre-receive/post-receive hook shims that export PORTITOR_CONFIG and exec the matching portitor subcommand.

requirements.title: Register a fingerprint->role in an existing repo config (add-role)
requirements.description: `portitor add-role --repo <name> --role <role> --fingerprint SHA256:... [--pub <file>]` is a re-runnable init step that edits an already-provisioned repos.d/<name>.json (config.ReposDir(), PORTITOR_REPOS_DIR-aware; ValidName-guarded so it stays inside the registry dir). It validates the fingerprint syntax + role name, sets Roles[fingerprint]=role (add, or overwrite a different mapping; identical binding is an idempotent no-op) while preserving every other config field, and — when --pub names the matching public key AND the role is a signing role — appends its `<role> namespaces="git" <keytype> <keydata>` line to the config's allowed_signers file (fingerprint-checked against the pub via ssh-keygen -lf, deduped; the file is created at mode 0644 if it does not yet exist). Signing-vs-identity classification is a closed denylist: identity-only roles are exactly {merger} and every other role name is a signing role. An identity-only role (merger) passed with --pub is refused, since its key must never gain commit-signing trust; likewise an overwrite that re-binds a fingerprint already present in allowed_signers to merger is refused. The config is written atomically (temp file + rename) and re-validated with config.Validate after the write; a missing config, a present-but-invalid --repo (ValidName failure, exit 2 usage error), an unreadable/malformed --pub file or ssh-keygen failure, a fingerprint/pub mismatch, or a validation problem fails the command loudly and leaves the config untouched. It never writes private key material.

requirements.title: Reject the whole push atomically with all violations
requirements.description: pre-receive runs once per push over git's object quarantine. portitor collects every violation across all refs/commits and, if any exist, exits non-zero printing a complete, actionable report to stderr (surfaced to the pusher as remote: lines); exits 0 only when there are zero violations.

requirements.title: Delegate cryptography to git
requirements.description: Signature verification is performed by `git verify-commit` against allowed_signers; portitor orchestrates and decides. Keeps the trust in git, not in portitor's own crypto.

requirements.title: Deterministic verification environment
requirements.description: The gate's verdict is a function of the push, the repo, and the config only. Every portitor git subprocess disables replace-object substitution (core.useReplaceRefs=false); the gate's fact-gathering calls mask global/system git config (GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null); the allowedSignersFile pin is passed unconditionally (an empty configured path makes every signature %G? == U, so a missing trust root deterministically rejects, never falls back to ambient config). Every subprocess (git, gh, ssh-keygen) runs under a deadline; a hung or failed check rejects, never accepts.

requirements.title: Validate hook stdin before use
requirements.description: pre/post-receive stdin lines must parse as '<old> <new> <ref>' with each SHA exactly 40 hex chars or all-zero and the ref 'refs/'-prefixed with no control bytes; any malformed line rejects the whole push. Configured remote names are validated (non-empty, no leading '-', no whitespace/control) before reaching git argv.

requirements.title: Gate is a pure, isolation-testable function
requirements.description: Check(repoDir, updates, cfg) -> ([]Violation, error) shells out only to git for facts, so it is testable with ephemeral throwaway keys and a temp bare repo — no real credentials or hardware.

components.name: Gate
components.description: Check() evaluates ref updates against the policy and returns all violations. Owns default-branch derivation, new-commit enumeration, per-commit signature verification + fingerprint->role attribution via git, and role-gated content rules.

components.name: Forwarding
components.description: Forward() pushes each accepted, non-default, non-deletion ref update to the configured upstream remote with the proxy's credentials. ForwardConfig {UpstreamRemote, DefaultBranch}; the default branch is never forwarded.

components.name: PreReceiveCommand
components.description: The `portitor pre-receive` subcommand: parses and validates stdin ref updates, loads settings from the PORTITOR_CONFIG JSON file, runs Gate.Check, and maps the result to an exit code + stderr report. The gate-integrity fields (default_branch, allowed_signers, roles, rules) come solely from the config file — they have NO env override, so a stray env var can never weaken the gate. Only the operational forwarding fields honor env (PORTITOR_UPSTREAM_REMOTE, PORTITOR_UPSTREAM_SLUG); the repo dir comes from GIT_DIR.

components.name: PostReceiveCommand
components.description: The `portitor post-receive` subcommand: parses stdin ref updates, loads the same settings, runs gate.Forward, and reports each forwarded/failed ref (non-zero exit if any forward failed).

components.name: InitRepoCommand
components.description: The `portitor init-repo` subcommand: creates the gated bare repo, optionally mirrors/seeds from upstream, and installs the pre-receive/post-receive hook shims wired to PORTITOR_CONFIG.

components.name: AddRoleCommand
components.description: The `portitor add-role` subcommand: loads repos.d/<name>.json (config.ReposDir(), PORTITOR_REPOS_DIR-aware), upserts the fingerprint->role binding in Roles, optionally appends a signing role's --pub key to allowed_signers (fingerprint-checked with ssh-keygen -lf, deduped), writes the config atomically (temp file + rename, mode preserved), and runs config.Validate afterwards. Malformed fingerprint/role, a missing config, a pub/fingerprint mismatch, or --pub for an identity-only role are loud errors; an identical re-run is an idempotent no-op.

components.name: Container
components.description: Deployment: a multi-stage Dockerfile builds the static portitor binary and runs it in a minimal Alpine image as a key-only git-over-SSH server (user 'git', repos on the /srv/git volume). deploy/entrypoint.sh generates host keys, installs AGENT_AUTHORIZED_KEY, and runs sshd under tini. The proxy is its own container, separate from the agent image.
